title: Negative Stain Transmission Electron Microscopy of ATG9A Vesicles
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eg9el","text":"This protocol details the description of Negative Stain Transmission Electron Microscopy of ATG9A Vesicles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5e35q","text":"Materials:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9p15j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4g9qm","text":"Carbon-coated copper-palladium EM grids","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2dfo3","text":"Glow discharge unit","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9hj3p","text":"2% (w/v) uranyl acetate solution (freshly prepared)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"635b5","text":"Filter paper","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b869e","text":"FEI Morgagni 268D transmission electron microscope","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"j9rp","text":"Morada 11-megapixel CCD camera (Olympus)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2m6ef","text":"ImageJ (RRID:SCR_003070; https://imagej.net/)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":25,"length":20}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"659F32F26EBC11F08A690A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://imagej.net/)"}}}}
steps: Glow discharge grids for 00:01:00 immediately before sample application to increase hydrophilicity.
Apply purified ATG9A vesicle suspension to the glow-discharged grid.
Stain by applying 5 µL of 2% uranyl acetate solution to the grid.
Repeat the staining step once more with fresh 2% uranyl acetate.
Store dried grids under vacuum until imaging.
Image stained grids using a FEI Morgagni 268D transmission electron microscope equipped with a tungsten filament emitter , operated at 80 kV .
Images were acquired at 44,000× magnification using an 11- megapixel Morada CCD camera (Olympus).
Analyze acquired micrographs using ImageJ (RRID:SCR_003070; https://imagej.net/).
creator: Elisabeth Holzer
